STATE OF CALIFORNIA-RESOUR~';:S AGENCY 
DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY 
1416 NINTH STREET 
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 
Telephone: 916-445-9144 
Mr. Jim McManus 
Chief Investigator 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Mr. McManus: 
EDMUND G. BROWN 
August 11, 1981 
Ll 
The California Department of Forestry is currently contracting with Solem and 
Associates and Vis/Arts Films for a major mass media fire prevention program. 
We have jointly produced and circulated a number of radio and television public 
serV1ce announcements which are now being released. 
During the taping of one of the television spots in Los Angeles, Assemblyman 
Art Torres was contacted, and volunteered to do a thirty second spot in Spanish. 
It follows the same theme as the major campaign: "Wildfires, We can't stop them 
without you~" and stresses what individuals can do around their dwellings, as 
well as while in the wildland, to prevent fires. 
We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting 
the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would 
be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district 
or broadcasting area. 
If you have any further questions, or would like to see the spot, please call 
me at 445-9144. 
Sincerely, 
kj~ 
Assistant to the Director 
sd 
CONSERVA liON IS WISE USE -
KEEP CALIFORNIA GREEN AND GOLDEN

sections.question: We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district or broadcasting area.
sections.question_synthetic: May the California Department of Forestry broadcast a public service announcement featuring Assemblyman Art Torres, and are there geographic restrictions on broadcasting such an announcement based on his district?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the Department of Forestry to FPPC Chief Investigator. The document ID 81A047 and year 1981 appear to reference the FPPC's response document, but the provided text contains primarily the original request letter. The document contains some OCR artifacts and scanning quality issues that slightly affect confidence. No formal CONCLUSION or FPPC response section is present in the provided text.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district or broadcasting area.

We have jointly produced and circulated a number of radio and television public serV1ce announcements which are now being released. During the taping of one of the television spots in Los Angeles, Assemblyman Art Torres was contacted, and volunteered to do a thirty second spot in Spanish. embedding.summary: The California Department of Forestry requests an FPPC opinion regarding whether they may broadcast a fire prevention public service announcement featuring Assemblyman Art Torres without geographic restrictions based on his legislative district.
